raw_text:
EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
The business reported a net favourable variance of -£379 (-3.6%) against the full-period budget of £10,665, with actual spend of £10,286. Within the significant lines, two cost lines overran materially — an accelerated marketing campaign and an unplanned emergency HVAC repair — while Subscriptions recorded a full £1,200 underspend whose cause remains unexplained by available evidence. The net position across significant lines is an overspend of £253 (+11.0% on those lines), partially absorbed by the Subscriptions saving.

LINE COMMENTARY

Advertising & Marketing | +£667 (+66.7%) | Budget: £1,000 | Actual: £1,667
The Q2 spring campaign was pulled forward from March into February on agency recommendation to capture a weather-sensitive launch window, with MD sign-off obtained on 1 February. The additional £667 above budget reflects this timing shift, and a campaign performance report from 20 February confirms the March budget has been reduced to £0 to offset, leaving net full-quarter spend expected to be on plan.
Sources
- Slack - 2024-11-23T12:15:00Z - slack-advertising_&_marketing-broad - "moving the Q2 spring campaign launch to this month. Client approval came in early… Total Feb commitment will be ~£1,600 vs the £1,000 March plan. Approvals from MD signed off."
- Gmail - 2024-11-23T18:15:00Z - gmail-advertising_&_marketing-broad - "MD approval for Q2 campaign acceleration. Spring campaign moved from March to February at agency recommendation — weather-sensitive launch window."
- Calendar - 2024-11-23T22:15:00Z - calendar-advertising_&_marketing-broad - "Spring campaign kick-off moved to 5 February. Agency briefed on 1 February per Slack decision."
- Gmail - 2024-11-23T18:45:00Z - gmail-advertising_&_marketing-narrow - "February campaign performance report. Spend £1,667. March budget reduced to £0 to offset. Net full-quarter spend on plan."

Repairs Maintenance | +£786 (+786.3%) | Budget: £100 | Actual: £886
An emergency HVAC failure in the server room on 14 February triggered an unplanned callout from FastAir Engineering, with the invoice totalling £886.30 inclusive of parts and emergency labour, approved by the CFO on the same day. A building insurance claim has been submitted; the outcome is pending, so any potential recovery is not yet recognised in the ledger and requires confirmation before it can be treated as an offset.
Sources
- Slack - 2024-11-19T12:15:00Z - slack-repairs_maintenance-broad - "HVAC unit in server room failed overnight. Building manager called emergency engineer. Quote came in at £886. CFO approved. Works completed today."
- Gmail - 2024-11-19T18:15:00Z - gmail-repairs_maintenance-broad - "Invoice from FastAir Engineering — emergency HVAC repair 14 Feb 2026. £886.30 inclusive of parts and emergency callout rate. CFO approved same day."
- Gmail - 2024-11-19T18:45:00Z - gmail-repairs_maintenance-narrow - "Building insurance claim submitted — outcome pending but expense recognised in February."

Subscriptions | -£1,200 (-100.0%) | Budget: £1,200 | Actual: £0
Actual spend was nil against a £1,200 budget, representing a full underspend. No context was found across Slack, Gmail, or any other source to explain whether subscriptions were cancelled, deferred, or simply not yet invoiced. No context found — recommend review.
Sources
- Slack - N/A - slack-subscriptions-broad - No evidence returned.
- Slack - N/A - slack-subscriptions-narrow - No evidence returned.
- Gmail - N/A - gmail-subscriptions-broad - No evidence returned.
- Gmail - N/A - gmail-subscriptions-narrow - No evidence returned.

INSIGNIFICANT VARIANCES
The following lines each fell below the significance threshold and were not subject to detailed investigation. No commentary is provided, but amounts are listed for completeness.
- Sales | +£506 (+8.4%)
- Purchases | -£500 (-100.0%)
- Audit & Accountancy fees | -£3 (-6.1%)
- Bank Fees | -£15 (-50.0%)
- Cleaning | -£1 (-0.8%)
- Entertainment - 0% | -£50 (-100.0%)
- Entertainment-100% business | +£13 (+0.0%)
- General Expenses | -£254 (-211.7%)
- Light Power Heating | +£13 (+14.9%)
- Motor Vehicle Expenses | -£71 (-54.6%)
- Postage Freight Courier | -£50 (-100.0%)
- Printing Stationery | -£20 (-100.0%)
- Rent | -£46 (-4.4%)
- Telephone Internet | -£6 (-13.3%)
- Travel National | -£150 (-100.0%)
